arts.ttl: Why Daniel Farke will be suspended for Leeds United's huge title clash with Sheffield United
arts.sum: Leeds United boss Daniel Farke will not be on the touchline for next week's huge Championship clash with Sheffield United. It means Farke's men are now seven points clear at the top of the Championship after Burnley dropped points at Preston North End. With 13 games to go, Leeds are in a great position for a return to the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Regis Le Bris hits the nail on the head with Leeds United assessment after late Sunderland loss
arts.sum: Head coach Regis Le Bris praised the impact of Leeds United's substitutes following Sunderland's 2-1 defeat at Elland Road this evening. Le Bris told Sky Sports: "It's a shame to concede the second goal like we did at the end of the game.

arts.ttl: Leeds return to the top after super-sub Struijk strikes twice to sink Sunderland
arts.sum: The news that a national poetry centre is to be established in Leeds proved the source of considerable pride in West Yorkshire on Monday. By 10pm on a bitterly cold night at Elland Road any notions that Leeds United would capture the mood courtesy of a suitably cadenced performance had been thoroughly disabused as Daniel Farke’s side scrapped their way to a dramatic last-gasp win after Wilson Isidor had given Sunderland the lead. Yet if it was hardly poetic as substitute Pascal Struijk’s two goals helped return Leeds to the top of the Championship, two points ahead of Sheffield United, seven points clear of third-placed Burnley and 10 in front of Régis Le Bris’s side. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 'Extraordinary scenes!' Late, LATE Struijk show earns Leeds HUGE win over Sunderland
arts.sum: Highlights of the Sky Bet Championship match between Leeds United and Sunderland.

arts.ttl: Sub Struijk strikes twice late on as Leeds hit back to beat Sunderland
arts.sum: Pascal Struijk scored twice as Leeds staged a stunning late comeback to beat Sunderland 2-1 to return to the top of the Championship.

arts.ttl: Leeds United player ratings vs Sunderland with Struijk and Rothwell super subs
arts.sum: The hosts went into half-time a goal down after Wilson Isidor found the net on 32 minutes after getting the wrong side of Ethan Ampadu. Pascal Struijk headed home from a brilliant Joe Rodon cross as the substitutes combined brilliantly.

arts.ttl: Pascal Struijk scores last-gasp winner against Sunderland to send Leeds top
arts.sum: The Dutch defender and Joe Rothwell came on with 20 minutes remaining and the pair combined for both of the Whites’ two late goals.

arts.ttl: Leeds United team news vs Sunderland with calls made on Ilia Gruev and Joe Rothwell
arts.sum: Starting XI: Meslier; Bogle, Ampadu, Rodon, Firpo; Tanaka, Gruev; James, Aaronson, Solomon; Piroe. It means Pascal Struijk is named on the bench again and awaits his first league start since New Year's Day. The centre-back played a full 90 minutes against Millwall in the FA Cup earlier this month, having returned from his hamstring injury, but he has not started since.

arts.ttl: How to watch Leeds United vs Sunderland - TV channel, live stream details, highlights - Leeds Live
arts.sum: Leeds United are under no illusion about the importance of tonight's match-up as they prepare to welcome AFC Sunderland to Elland Road in the Championship. No one else in the community will be able to see your personal information and you will only receive messages from the Leeds Live sports team.

arts.ttl: Leeds United v Sunderland: Championship – live
arts.sum: Championship updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off Live scores | Latest table | Football Daily | Mail Michael 2 min: Huge chance for Sunderland and humungous double save from Meslier! Ballard comes out of defence with all the poise and swagger of a young Jonathan Woodgate, releases Le Fée on the left wing. The Frenchman squares the ball to Bellingham on the edge of the area – completely free after a Gruev slip – but the Sunderland midfielder doesn’t beat Meslier with his shot and the Leeds keeper gets quickly to his feet to block the rebound! We’re off in Yorkshire. I have just learned Leeds’ Meslier and Sunderland’s Le Fée were friends at school in Lorient, France. Continue reading...
